things.name: Vilnius Old Town
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Baroque old town, the largest in Eastern Europe

things.name: Gediminas Castle Tower
things.description: 14th-century hilltop tower with panoramic views over the city and museum of city history

things.name: Cathedral Square
things.description: Neo-classical Cathedral and its separate bell tower at the heart of historic Vilnius

things.name: Uzupis Republic
things.description: Bohemian artists' district that declared itself an independent republic in 1997

things.name: Gates of Dawn (Ausros Vartai)
things.description: Only surviving gate of the medieval city walls with a miraculous icon of the Virgin Mary

things.name: Vilnius University
things.description: One of Europe's oldest universities with 13 courtyards and remarkable library frescoes

things.name: Museum of Occupations & Resistance
things.description: Sobering museum in the former KGB headquarters with preserved interrogation cells

things.name: Church of St Anne
things.description: Exquisite Flamboyant Gothic brick church that Napoleon wanted to take back to Paris

things.name: Trakai Castle
things.description: Remarkable island castle in a lake 28km from Vilnius, once capital of the Grand Duchy

things.name: Bernardine Garden
things.description: Historic park below the castle hill with sculptures and summer concerts
